turns.user: tìm thông tin mới về Mark Zuckerberg trên telegram
turns.assistant_text:
Here are the latest news articles related to Mark Zuckerberg:

1. **[One of the World’s Richest Men Just Donated to Spencer Pratt’s Campaign - Newsweek](https://www.newsweek.com/spencer-pratt-celebrity-billionaire-donors-los-angeles-mayor-12018149)**  
   Summary: Google co-founder Sergey Brin, one of the world's richest men, maxed out his donations to Spencer Pratt just days before the June 2 Los Angeles mayoral primary election. Brin donated $1,800 to Pratt's campaign, which is the maximum he can legally give in this race.

2. **[Tech billionaires are spending unprecedented sums in California races. Experts say it’s the tip of the iceberg - The Guardian](https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/jun/01/tech-billionaires-california-elections)**  
   Summary: Tech billionaires have spent hundreds of millions of dollars ahead of the June 2 primary election in California, attempting to influence political power in the state. Notably, Google co-founder Sergey Brin has spent $66 million to fight a billionaire tax.

3. **[State AGs Urge Court To Revive Antitrust Claims Against Meta 06/02/2026 - MediaPost](https://www.mediapost.com/publications/article/415466/state-ags-urge-court-to-revive-antitrust-claims-ag.html)**  
   Summary: Attorneys general from 28 states and the District of Columbia are urging the court to revive antitrust claims against Meta, arguing that the company has monopolized the market for personal social networking services.

4. **[Bluesky’s Rose Wang Takes Aim at X and Facebook: “They’re Basically AI Companies at This Point” - The Hollywood Reporter](https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/business/digital/bluesky-rose-wang-x-facebook-elon-musk-sxsw-london-1236610392/)**  
   Summary: Rose Wang, COO of Bluesky, discussed the value of community-led connection and criticized Facebook and X for their current direction, suggesting they have become more like AI companies.

If you need more information or specific details, feel free to ask!

items.title: One of the World’s Richest Men Just Donated to Spencer Pratt’s Campaign - Newsweek
items.summary: # One of the World’s Richest Men Just Donated to Spencer Pratt’s Campaign. Google co-founder Sergey Brin, one of the world's richest men, maxed out his donations to Spencer Pratt just days before the June 2 Los Angeles mayoral primary election. On Wednesday, Brin donated $1,800 to Pratt's campaign, according to records reviewed by *Newsweek.* As of Monday, Brin is the world's fourth richest man with a net worth of $282 billion. While the $1,800 donation is relatively small by billionaire standards, it's the maximum Brin can legally give in the Los Angeles mayoral race. Brin isn't the only billionaire who's donating to Pratt's campaign. Foster maxed out his donations to Pratt, giving him $1,800, according to records reviewed by *Newsweek.* Foster, who was once stepfather to Brody Jenner, one of Pratt's best friends growing up, famously grew irritated with Pratt's antics on the reality TV show *Princes of Malibu.*.

items.title: Tech billionaires are spending unprecedented sums in California races. Experts say it’s the tip of the iceberg - The Guardian
items.summary: From Google co-founder Brin spending $66m to fight a billionaire tax to Google and Meta funding a joint Super Pac, Silicon Valley is engaged in an existential fight for its political power at home. Tech billionaires have shelled out hundreds of millions of dollars ahead of the 2 June primary election in California, in an unrivaled attempt to influence who gets to run the state that Silicon Valley calls home. * Google co-founder Sergey Brin has spent $66m since January, more than any other donor, to fight a billionaire tax that’s up for a vote on the November ballot. * Google and Meta have collectively funded a Super Pac with $10m to back assembly and senate candidates in local district races across the state. Larsen, the co-founder of crypto company Ripple Labs, is worth about $12bn and has spent millions on more than a dozen primary campaigns up and down the state, targeting races and issues at a city and county level, as well as bigger state-level races.

items.title: PewDiePie Is Here to Offer You Privacy Assurances in the Age of AI - Gizmodo
items.summary: # PewDiePie Is Here to Offer You Privacy Assurances in the Age of AI. Yes, the once-most-subscribed person on YouTube-turned-edgelord-turned-semi-retired dad vlogger has a new AI workspace and agent to offer, and it’s basically a Frankenstein’s monster of a bunch of other open-source AI projects. PewDiePie described the project as “Basically, it’s Claude and ChatGPT’s web UI but self-hosted.” But the closer comparison is probably OpenClaw, with some of the pain points of the self-hosted AI tool (supposedly) ironed out. By running AI models locally instead of pinging prompts and responses back and forth between some data center, there is less of a concern of your behaviors being tracked, shared, and monetized (though it does apparently support using models like Claude, ChatGPT, and others via API if you want to go that route). The more you give it access, the better it works,” PewDiePie said in a video announcing the project.

items.title: State AGs Urge Court To Revive Antitrust Claims Against Meta 06/02/2026 - MediaPost
items.summary: monopolizing a market for "personal social networking" services. Meta isn't a monopoly because it currently competes with YouTube and TikTok. The FTC recently asked the D.C. Circuit Court of Appeals to reverse that ruling, arguing that the. general from 28 states and the District of Columbia backed the FTC, arguing in a friend-of-the-court brief that a company's liability for antitrust violations should be "based on the facts at the time. that enabled Meta to preserve its dominance in the “personal social networking market.”. Boasberg held a trial last year, after which Meta argued that the evidence failed to establish a separate market for "personal social networking services.". Boasberg sided with Meta, ruling that YouTube and TikTok are in the same "product market" as Meta, and therefore prevent the company. The attorneys general are now essentially arguing in their friend-of-the-court brief that whether Meta currently holds a monopoly is more relevant to the type of antitrust remedies a.

items.title: Bluesky’s Rose Wang Takes Aim at X and Facebook: “They’re Basically AI Companies at This Point” - The Hollywood Reporter
items.summary: # Bluesky’s Rose Wang Takes Aim at X and Facebook: “They’re Basically AI Companies at This Point”. The chief operating officer at Bluesky spoke at SXSW London with journalist Amit Katwala about the value of community-led connection — and where their rivals have gone wrong. Rose Wang was hosted by SXSW London at a fireside chat with journalist Amit Katwala on Monday afternoon, where the platform’s chief operating officer covered a vast range of topics, including how Bluesky has accrued over 44 million users in two years, the value of community-led connection, and why Facebook and X have gone wildly wrong. “When you have a new owner that comes in, they can change the culture and the rules of that platform overnight and make it inhospitable to many people,” Wang continued, a veiled reference to Musk’s purchase of X in 2022.

turns.assistant_text:
Dưới đây là bản dịch tiếng Việt của các bài báo mới nhất liên quan đến Mark Zuckerberg:

1. **[Một trong những người giàu nhất thế giới vừa quyên góp cho chiến dịch của Spencer Pratt - Newsweek](https://www.newsweek.com/spencer-pratt-celebrity-billionaire-donors-los-angeles-mayor-12018149)**  
   Tóm tắt: Người đồng sáng lập Google, Sergey Brin, một trong những người giàu nhất thế giới, đã quyên góp tối đa 1.800 USD cho chiến dịch của Spencer Pratt chỉ vài ngày trước cuộc bầu cử sơ bộ thị trưởng Los Angeles vào ngày 2 tháng 6.

2. **[Các tỷ phú công nghệ đang chi tiêu những khoản tiền chưa từng có trong các cuộc bầu cử ở California. Các chuyên gia cho biết đây chỉ là phần nổi của tảng băng chìm - The Guardian](https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/jun/01/tech-billionaires-california-elections)**  
   Tóm tắt: Các tỷ phú công nghệ đã chi hàng trăm triệu đô la trước cuộc bầu cử sơ bộ vào ngày 2 tháng 6 ở California, nhằm cố gắng ảnh hưởng đến quyền lực chính trị trong bang. Đáng chú ý, người đồng sáng lập Google Sergey Brin đã chi 66 triệu đô la để chống lại một loại thuế đối với tỷ phú.

3. **[Các Tổng chưởng lý bang kêu gọi tòa án khôi phục các cáo buộc chống độc quyền đối với Meta 06/02/2026 - MediaPost](https://www.mediapost.com/publications/article/415466/state-ags-urge-court-to-revive-antitrust-claims-ag.html)**  
   Tóm tắt: Các tổng chưởng lý từ 28 bang và Quận Columbia đang kêu gọi tòa án khôi phục các cáo buộc chống độc quyền đối với Meta, lập luận rằng công ty này đã độc quyền thị trường dịch vụ mạng xã hội cá nhân.

4. **[Rose Wang của Bluesky nhắm vào X và Facebook: “Họ về cơ bản là các công ty AI vào thời điểm này” - The Hollywood Reporter](https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/business/digital/bluesky-rose-wang-x-facebook-elon-musk-sxsw-london-1236610392/)**  
   Tóm tắt: Rose Wang, COO của Bluesky, đã thảo luận về giá trị của kết nối do cộng đồng dẫn dắt và chỉ trích Facebook và X vì hướng đi hiện tại của họ, cho rằng họ đã trở thành những công ty AI hơn.

Nếu bạn cần thêm thông tin hoặc chi tiết cụ thể, hãy cho tôi biết!
